title: South Korea: Gradual CPI rise keeps BoK cautious – ING
body: ING’s Senior Economist Min Joo Kang notes that South Korean inflation accelerated in April on higher energy prices, but government measures such as food vouchers, a gasoline price cap and frozen utility tariffs helped limit the increase.

title: Fed’s Goolsbee: Productivity's impact on inflation and interest rates could go in either direction
body: Austan Goolsbee, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Chicago, spoke at the 2026 Milken Institute Global Conference in California on Wednesday. He stated that the impact of rising productivity on inflation remains an active topic of debate among the Fed.

title: China: Managed trade truce prospects – Standard Chartered
body: Standard Chartered economists Carol Liao and Shuang Ding argue that both the US and China are incentivised to keep their bilateral relationship stable as President Trump’s 14–15 May visit approaches.

title: WTI Oil slumps on US-Iran diplomatic progress, EIA inventory drawdown
body: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil tumbles on Wednesday, trading around $92.30 at the time of writing, down 7.62% on the day as investors rapidly unwind geopolitical risk premiums following reports of a potential agreement between Washington and Tehran.

title: Dow Jones Industrial Average rallies over 1% on Iran deal hopes
body: US equities pushed higher on Wednesday as risk appetite returned on signs that the US-Iran conflict could be heading toward a broader resolution. The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) added roughly 540 points to close above 49,800 after testing levels near 50,000 intraday.

title: LatAm: Hedging demand rises with Banxico in focus – BNY
body: BNY’s Geoff Yu argues Latin American FX and equities now represent a single crowded ‘total return’ trade, with all regional currencies still overheld while bond holdings begin to reverse unevenly.

title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD rally pauses below 50-day SMA after intraday surge
body: Silver (XAG/USD) gains traction on Wednesday as renewed optimism surrounding a potential US-Iran peace deal triggers a sharp decline in the US Dollar (USD) and Oil prices. At the time of writing, XAG/USD is trading around 77, up over 5.50% on the day.

title: GBP/USD surges as US-Iran deal hopes hammer US Dollar
body: GBP/USD rises by over 0.59% on Wednesday after an Axios report revealed that the US and Iran are closing in on a deal to end the war. The US Dollar (USD) fell on the news, even though US jobs data crushed estimates, which could prompt the Federal Reserve (Fed) to focus on inflation.

title: EUR/USD rallies on US-Iran optimism, upside capped by lingering uncertainty
body: EUR/USD trades higher on Wednesday as renewed optimism surrounding a potential US-Iran peace deal pressures the US Dollar (USD) and lifts the Euro (EUR).

title: AUD/USD rallies as Iran-US deal hopes outweigh strong ADP jobs data
body: AUD/USD surges near the 0.7240 price region, supported by improving risk sentiment after Axios reported that the United States (US) and Iran are moving closer to a deal aimed at ending the conflict.

title: USD: Peace progress and Oil slide weigh – MUFG
body: MUFG’s Derek Halpenny notes that renewed hopes for a peace deal with Iran and a sharp drop in Brent crude Oil are pressuring the US Dollar (USD), with risk appetite supported by strong global equities and AI-driven gains.

title: SEK: Positive real rates support currency outlook – BBH
body: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad highlights that USD/SEK has fallen on broad Dollar weakness while Sweden’s April disinflation surprised to the downside, reducing Riksbank hike expectations.

title: XAG/USD Price analysis: Silver recovers its spark, next hurdle at $80
body: Silver edged higher on Wednesday, as optimism put a halt to US Dollar (USD) demand. Market players welcomed headlines indicating that the United States (US) and Iran were closing in on a deal to end the Middle East conflict.

title: The Fed Warsh inherits: Divided and with its independence in doubt
body: The Federal Reserve is the central bank of the world’s most powerful economy, and prides itself on its independence. But is it? Or, better said, will it continue to be under Kevin Warsh?

title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Seems vulnerable amid suspected JPY intervention, weaker USD
body: The USD/JPY pair attracts heavy intraday selling on Wednesday and dives to the 155.00 psychological mark, or the lowest level since February 24, though it lacks follow-through.

title: The RBI’s problem with the Indian Rupee has no easy fix
body: The Indian Rupee’s drama continues despite the RBI’s recent aggressive interventions and regulatory curbs. While these actions have caused the sharpest single-day gains in years, their effect has been wiped out in a matter of days. So why is every RBI move failing to break the Rupee’s downtrend?

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD rebounds on Mideast peace hopes, will it last?
body: Gold is rallying hard in Asia on Wednesday, reclaiming the $4,600 barrier while extending its recovery from over one-month lows of $4,501 set on Tuesday.

title: UK 30-year gilts hit 5.78%, the highest since 1998: what is being priced in?
body: UK 30-year gilts traded as high as 5.78% on Tuesday, the highest level since 1998, while 10-year yields topped 5.10% with markets pricing in nearly three-quarter-point Bank of England (BoE) rate hikes this year.

title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra consolidation on the cards
body: The Aussie Dollar seems to have embarked on a consolidative phase, with AUD/USD finding support around the 0.7100 neighbourhood while the upside appears capped by the YTD tops just below 0.7200 the figure.

title: Gold Price Forecast: XAU/USD bounces from critical threshold, risk skews to the downside
body: Spot Gold posts modest intraday gains on Tuesday, changing hands at around $4,550 per troy ounce. The precious metal quickly started the week with a weak tone, as tensions in the Middle East escalated.

title: Iran war: Everything you need to know about peace talks
body: Donald Trump has refocused his efforts on peace talks after a shock pause to 'Project Freedom' - the US effort to safely shepherd ships through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Megatsunami bigger than Empire State Building triggered by climate change
body: A tsunami at a popular tourist spot in Alaska was the second highest ever recorded and it was "unbelievably lucky" no one was hurt, researchers have said.

title: Pilot dies after firefighting ‌plane tackling major forest blaze crashes
body: A pilot has been killed after his firefighting ‌plane crashed while battling a major blaze that has destroyed 200 hectares of protected forest in Poland.

title: Has Trump just made another mess in the Middle East?
Student, 13, arrested after two killed in Brazilian school shooting
body: A 13-year-old student has been arrested after a shooting at a school in Brazil left two women dead.
